title: arctostaphylos glandulosa subsp. howellii
intro: Arctostaphylos glandulosa subsp. howellii, also known as Howell's manzanita, is a subspecies of evergreen shrub that belongs to the heath family, native to California and Oregon in the United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, such as wound healing and immune system support. Howell's manzanita is also rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
